x-enumeration-literals.name: Physical Safe Keeping
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The institution stores tangible assets in secure physical facilities such as vaults.

Comment:
Example:
Storage of paper certificates, gold bars, or legal documents.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Electronic Safe Keeping
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Assets exist only in electronic form and are recorded in digital systems or central securities depositories.

Comment:
Example:
Government bonds or equities held in electronic book-entry form.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Custody Safe Keeping
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Assets are held by the institution on behalf of the client, with full administrative services such as settlement, income collection, and reporting.

Comment:
Example:
A bank holds a clients stocks and bonds in a custody account and manages dividend collection.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Segregated Safe Keeping
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Client assets are kept separate from the institutions own assets and from other clients assets, ensuring clear ownership and protection.

Comment:
Example:
High-value securities held in a dedicated account under the clients name.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Omnibus Safe Keeping
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Assets of multiple clients are pooled together in a single account, but ownership is tracked internally by the institution.

Comment:
Example:
A broker holds shares for many clients in one combined account but maintains records of individual ownership.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Collateral Safe Keeping
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Assets are held specifically as collateral to secure obligations, with restrictions on their use until obligations are fulfilled.

Comment:
Example:
Securities pledged against a loan and held by the bank.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Third-Party Safe Keeping
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Assets are held by an external custodian or sub-custodian on behalf of the institution and its clients.

Comment:
Example:
International securities held through a global custodian bank.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Restricted Safe Keeping
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Assets are held with specific restrictions or controls, limiting access, transfer, or usage based on legal or contractual conditions.

Comment:
Example:
Assets frozen due to legal disputes or regulatory requirements.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Safekeeping with Corporate Actions Support
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Assets are held with additional services to manage corporate actions such as dividends, splits, or voting rights.

Comment:
Example:
A bank processes stock splits and proxy voting for held securities.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Precious Assets Safe Keeping
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Specialized storage for high-value physical assets requiring enhanced security and insurance.

Comment: 
Example:
Jewelry, rare coins, or precious metals stored in high-security vaults.
